data.title: Weak July jobs report with just 73,000 added, biggest downward revisions in 2 months since 2020. Economists and markets calling for lower rates, impacting Powell. Unemployment up to 4.2%, wages rising 3.9% year-over-year. Labor market data leads to pro-Trump sentiments.
data.description: In response to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's reluctance to confirm a September rate cut, the U.S. labor market showcased its need for one. The SPY ETF surpassed its support level following a weak July jobs report, where only 73,000 jobs were added, far below the expected 110,000. Additionally, there was a record downward revision of 258,000 jobs for May and June combined, the largest revision since 2020. Private-sector job growth was mainly in healthcare, while government payrolls decreased by 10,000. The unemployment rate rose to 4.2%, wages increased by 0.3% monthly and 3.9% annually. Economists and markets are now advocating for lower interest rates. President Donald Trump is also supporting this call. Manufacturing data remains weak, with overall labor data showing signs of decline. More information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Call to investigate Wake Forest for animal cruelty involving monkeys; group urges Forsyth County Sheriff & NC Attorney General to act; formal complaint filed citing potential harm to small monkeys.
data.description: A group of physicians nationwide has lodged a complaint against Wake Forest University in North Carolina, urging the Forsyth County Sheriff and the state's Attorney General to intervene in a scheduled course involving small monkeys. The nonprofit Physicians Committee for Responsible Medicine filed a formal complaint cited fears that the procedures would result in pain, suffering, injury, and possibly death for the animals. The committee is pushing for immediate action to prevent what they believe constitutes animal cruelty.

data.title: Global market for Pluggable Optics for Data Centers estimated at US$5.6B in 2024, projected to reach US$9.9B by 2030, growing at 9.8% CAGR. Comprehensive analysis report on market trends and forecasts.
data.description: Dublin - A new report on Pluggable Optics for Data Centers has been introduced to the market by ResearchAndMarkets.com. The global market size for these optics was valued at approximately US$5.6 billion in 2024. This detailed report offers a thorough examination of market patterns, factors propelling growth, and future projections to assist businesses in making well-informed decisions.

data.title: Blacksmith Medicines Co-Founder introduces novel chemistry platform targeting metalloenzymes at the 21st International Conference on Biological Inorganic Chemistry.
data.description: During a recent presentation, Blacksmith Medicines, Inc. unveiled their innovative chemistry platform for targeting metalloenzymes. The event also showcased FG-2101, a groundbreaking non-hydroxamate LpxC inhibitor. Blacksmith, a prominent biopharmaceutical company, shared these advancements in San Diego on Aug. 1, 2025. This novel approach holds great promise in the field, with FG-2101 being a noteworthy first-in-class inhibitor. The presentation shed light on Blacksmith's commitment to pioneering research and development in the biopharmaceutical sector, illustrating their dedication to cutting-edge solutions in healthcare.

data.title: Tractor Supply sponsors USDA's Great American Farmers Market in 2025 ahead of US's 250th anniversary, happening August 3-8 from 4-8 p.m.
data.description: BRENTWOOD, Tenn.-- Tractor Supply Company, the leading rural lifestyle retailer in the U.S., revealed its role as the primary sponsor for the U.S. Department of Agriculture’s 2025 Great American Farmers Market, honoring the country’s agricultural community ahead of the U.S.’s 250th anniversary in 2026. The market will run daily from August 3 to August 8, between 4 to 8 p.m.

data.title: Reddit Q2 revenue surges 78%, surpassing expectations. Shares rise after company reports 110 million daily active users and positive financial outlook. Analysts cautious despite growth.
data.description: Reddit Inc.'s stock surged following a successful second-quarter report, aligning with broader tech industry trends. Financial analysts noted Reddit's expanding user engagement and advertising growth, with over 110 million daily active unique users (DAUs). JPMorgan's Doug Anmuth raised Reddit's price target to $190, expressing potential for profitability by 2025. Guggenheim Securities' Michael Morris also acknowledged Reddit's positive performance. The company's potential for achieving 50% long-term adjusted EBITDA margins was highlighted, contributing to the optimistic outlook. Rallied in early trading, Reddit's Q2 performance has sparked interest and optimism among analysts.
